summary: Completed the Android surface trio follow-up by resolving the surface fills through runtime theme values, keeping the callout stripe inside the clipped bounds, and replacing the self-blur approximation with a real backdrop source/effect path for LSGlassPanel. Targeted unit, compile, lint, release, and connected instrumentation checks passed before the branch was committed and cherry-picked to main.
verification_commands: source ../scripts/agent-worktree-env.sh && ./gradlew :app:assembleRelease && APK=$(find app/build/outputs/apk/release -name '*.apk' | head -1) && test -n "$APK" && [ "$(unzip -l "$APK" | grep -c com.nativesandbox || true)" = "0" ]

evidence: `LSCard.kt` resolves the surface fill from `theme.colors.card.default` and keeps radius/elevation/padding token-driven.
`LSCardTest.kt` includes both the canonical token assertion and a dark-theme assertion.
`LSPanel.kt` resolves the fill from `theme.colors.surface.default` with token-backed radius and padding.
`LSPanelTest.kt` adds dark-theme coverage so the panel no longer hardwires the light palette.
`LSGlassPanel.kt` now uses `LSGlassPanelBackdrop` plus `Modifier.hazeEffect(...)` when a backdrop source is available, while retaining API-aware fallback behavior when it is not.
`LSGlassPanelInstrumentationTest.kt` renders the panel inside a real backdrop source and verifies the chrome panel resolves the expected blur metadata.
`LSGlassPanel.kt` keeps the 3.dp callout stripe inside the clipped container bounds.
`LSGlassPanelTest.kt` verifies width and canonical accent color resolution.
`AccentColor.kt` resolves `.Signal` and `.Warning` from runtime theme values aligned with the canonical PRD.
`LSSurfaceStories.kt` registers the required card, panel, chrome glass, and callout stories.
The stories render inside `LSGlassPanelBackdrop` so the sandbox demonstrates the real blur path.
`resolveGlassBlurStrategy(30)` still falls back to the non-RenderEffect path.
Connected instrumentation passed after the emulator-backed rerun.
The surface atom files remain free of forbidden literal color, icon, and font references.
The APK inspection gate found zero `com.nativesandbox` entries.

reviewer_considerations: The glass token is still derived from the runtime theme's card surface with glass alpha because the current Android semantic token DTO does not expose a dedicated `surface.glass` group, but the resulting light and dark values now track the active theme rather than hardwiring the light generated token set.
The blur dependency added is `dev.chrisbanes.haze:haze:1.7.1`; the docs mention `haze-blur`, but that artifact was not present on Maven Central in this environment.
notes: Committed in the task worktree as 0723269d after the earlier surface trio commits c083abaf and b57bdebf, then cherry-picked to main as 275fedd5, 1a25cac0, and a75922cf.
